SUBJECT: Request for Quotation (RFQ) – Reworking a G4 Galley 
 
The De Havilland Aircraft of Canada Limited, an aerospace leader (hereinafter referred to as "De 
Havilland"), is hereby inv iting suppliers (hereinafter referred to as "Bidder") to submit a 
commercial quotation for the supplying the engineering and reworking a G4 Galley (the “Work”) 
to be integrated in the Dash 8  Series-400 (hereinafter referred to as " Aircraft") in accordance 
with the provisions specified herein.  Bidders can bid on one or both statements for work.  De 
Havilland is requesting a Quote from Bidders to assist DHC in selling a refurbished Aircraft. 
 
De Havilland seeks a Bidder which will undertake for the first statement of work to develop, test, 
qualify, and for the second statement of work to manufacture to support the Aircraft certification. 
This also implies maintaining the Work at a very competitiv e level, while adapting it to  meet 
customer requirements. 
 
De Havilland is the owner of the design  and w here applicable, will give a license to use that 
design with the option to sub-license with De Havilland ’s approval, any of the  intellectual 
property rights required in the develop, certify, test, qualification, integration and manufacture of 
the Work.  
 
Bidder shall develop all concepts addressed in this Request for Quotation while recognizing that 
the attached documents constitutes De Havilland’s minimum requirements. Any other innovative 
ideas and improv ements, either technical or c ommercial, developed by Bidder wil l be closely 
analyzed by De Havilland, and the Bidder shall ensur e the feasibility of any new and in novative 
Quotations throughout deve lopment and production. Alternative Quotations are encouraged as 
De Havillan d continuously st rives to ensure it s products  remain h ighly competitive through 
effective and cost efficient imp rovements. Incomplete or partial Quotations will only be 
considered as an alternate solution. 
 
If applicable, one of the objectives of this feasibility project is to exchange ideas concerning the 
development of th e best tooling in its class suite d for the Work. At t his stage, De Havilland 
encourages ideas, c reativity and Bidder’s ability to s howcase its product as well as its end use 
application. 
 
The bid submission must be submitted no later than end of Monday, May 20th, 2024. 
 
All communications, including technical and commercial questions, related to this Request for 
Quotation must be submitted in writing only, using the address below. Any questions and 
answers of a general nature, which do not contain any confidential information, will be shared 
with all Bidders via email.

Background Information and Assumptions

title: PDF Page 6
content:
***  CONFIDENTIAL  *** PAGE 6 OF 13 
Due to the customer requested cabin configuration on MSN 4506 and the galley combination, 
there are no available space for an aft flight attendant handset.  It is suggested to retrofit galley 
part number 9654001-105 to add a flight attendant handset. 
DHC has a new G4 galley, P/N 9654001-105 in stock equipped with 2 Sell ovens, provisions for 
4 trolleys and 2 Service Units.  This statement of work is to rework the G4 galley to include a 
flight attendant handset, the handset is to be located at a similar location as the aft draft 
bulkhead, refer to the attachment section of this statement of work for  picture of the handset 
installed on the type spec left-hand side bulkhead.  This statement of work also removes the 
overhead light panel switches as the light switches are already located on the G3 galley. 
Pre-Requisite 
G3 Galley – Aft, Fwd facing with one Zodiac (TIA) coffee maker and one Aerolux Espresso 
Maker, Misc stowage, provisions for 4 Atlas half trolleys and provisions for 3 Service units. 
Equipment and furnishings – Passenger compartment, aft draft bulkhead, left-hand side with 
large aft pulling equipment drawer and grab handle. 
Equipment and furnishings – NextGen aft draft bulkhead, right-hand side with large aft pull 
drawer, curtain and assist handle. 
 
Statement of work 
Galley rework 
The cabin configuration for MSN 4506 (Ex-NOK) consists of an Aft lavatory, G3 and G4 galley, 
from the installed monuments, none are equipped or provisioned for a flight attendant handset. 
Refer to the LOPA posted in the Attachment section of this PR. For a 54-passenger 
configuration, 2 ovens are preferrable to cater hot meals to all passengers simultaneously.  As a 
result, it is suggested to rework G4 Galley part number 9654001-105 currently in stock to add a 
flight attendant handset, refer to Fig 1 and 2 for reference. 
The handset is to be located on the aft face of the G4 galley utilizing the inboard trolley area to 
install the handset.  A close out panel is to be installed at the inboard trolley area to create a 
mounting surface for the handset. A forward/aft panel is also to be installed to completely close 
out the area behind the front close out panel to prevent the stowage of miscellaneous items.  The 
flight attendant handset current installed on the right-hand side aft draft bulkhead, P/N 
AH2542AT03, be reused on the modified G4 galley. The right-hand side aft draft bulkhead will 
be replaced with an aft lavatory. Programs to decide if the aft draft bulkhead is to be salvaged for 
future use, if so, a new flight attendant handset is to be purchased for reinstallation at a later date. 
The G4 galley oven control panel currently included the overhead light control switches 
including the “ON” and “Dim/Bright” switch. A similar panel controlling the overhead lights is 
already installed on the G3 galley control panel. The G4 galley panel is to be either replaced with 
a new panel without the switches, P/N 9654701-505 or reworked to remove the four switches

title: PDF Page 7
content:
***  CONFIDENTIAL  *** PAGE 7 OF 13 
and breaker.  The aircraft wiring for the installation of the G4 galley is to exclude the provisions 
to the overhead light panel. Refer to Fig 3 for the existing control panel installed on the G4 
galley.  
Galley Installation 
The existing LHS aft draft bulkhead installed shall be removed from MSN 4506 and replaced 
with the reworked G4 galley with a flight attendant handset as described above. The cabin 
finishes including dado panel, sidewall, overhead bin, window blanking panel and ceiling panels 
shall be adjusted accordingly. The Emergency equipment installed in the LH large aft pulling 
drawer is to be adjusted and relocated to the G4 galley doghouse to match the TNZ emergency 
equipment installed per 82560601-606, sheet 159.  The G4 galley structural provisions shall be 
installed.  The dry carpet forward of the left-hand side aft draft bulkhead and the wet flooring 
installed in the aft galley area shall be adjusted to suit the G4 galley installation.  The AC 
contactor box in the LHS nacelle shall be installed including all wiring for AC and DC power 
and circuit breaker.  The wiring for the flight attendant handset shall be re-routed from the right-
hand side aft draft bulkhead to the G4 galley.  The galley equipment including 2 Atlas half size 
trolleys, 2 Service units, 2 sell ovens, 2 oven controllers, 2 oven inserts and 16 oven shelves are 
to be provided.  All galley related labels including maximum load limit labels and emergency 
equipment labels shall be installed. 
Impact 
Bidder 
Create rework engineering drawings for the G4 galley (handset installation and control panel 
rework) 
Perform rework to the G4 galley per the narrative work statement. 
Bidder to provide a complete G4 galley assembly ready for installation. 
Bidder to provide flammability reports, stress and weight reports. 
All associated paper with the certification of the galley is to be provided.

The revision of  a Supplier Approval Status as stated above  will result  in the supplier  being
